div.h1: Why cd Must Be a Shell Builtin
div.h3: Technical Analysis: Unix Process Isolation & PCB State Persistence
div.p: Engineering Guide: Reference the memory isolation boundary and Task Structure (PCB) transitions below.

g.text: ✅ Scenario B: Internal Shell Builtin Execution

tr.td: Parse file in current stack frame

ul.li: Kernel Stack: chdir() updates the fs->pwd field in the current task_struct.
Isolation Boundary: PID 1001 and PID 1000 occupy distinct memory segments via VM paging.
Persistence: Changes to PID 1001's process control block (PCB) do not propagate to the parent.
Data Flow: The only communication channel on exit is the 8-bit integer returned via waitpid().
Execution Context: The shell process executes the chdir() syscall within its own thread of execution.
State Change: The kernel directly modifies the caller's task_struct->fs->pwd pointer.
Stack Frame: Control returns to the shell's command loop, which now operates in the new environment.
Resource Efficiency: Zero overhead from process creation (fork/exec) or memory duplication.
